reasoning: The base rate of a new coronavirus pandemic in any given year is exceptionally low (<1%). The prompt asks if a new coronavirus pandemic (other than SARS-CoV-2) will be declared by the WHO between Feb 21, 2025 and Dec 31, 2025. It references a Feb 21, 2025 Newsweek article about a new bat coronavirus discovered in a Chinese lab (HKU5-CoV-2). However, further searches reveal this was merely an experimental lab finding that the virus can utilize human ACE2 receptors. The CDC immediately stated it is not a public health threat and has not infected humans (as of Feb 2025). Additional searches through December 2025 reveal no news of any new coronavirus outbreaks or a WHO declaration of a pandemic. The Polymarket price in late November 2025 was 0.01. Given that we are at the forecast date of December 7, 2025, and there have been no documented cases or outbreaks of a new coronavirus, there is no chance a new pandemic will be declared by the WHO before December 31, 2025. I am providing 0.02 as the minimum allowed probability.
